description:
# Why

`state_repo::plugins_tests::ensure_plugins_symlink_idempotent` (src/state_repo_plugins_tests.rs:49) fails intermittently when the full `cargo test` suite runs in parallel — roughly 1 run in 3. The panic is the first `ensure(root.path(), &url).unwrap()`: `ensure` returns Err under parallel load.

It passes deterministically otherwise:
- in isolation: `cargo test --lib ensure_plugins_symlink_idempotent`
- single-threaded: `--test-threads=1` gives state_repo:: 19/19, repeatedly.

So it is a parallel-execution race in `state_repo::ensure` (or the `hub_repo()` test fixture), not a logic bug. `ensure` shells out to git to clone a hub; the race is most likely a contended shared resource — a global git config, a leaked GIT_* env var, or a non-isolated path — surfacing only under concurrent git invocations.

# Scope
- Reproduce by running the full suite repeatedly.
- Find the shared/contended resource in `state_repo::ensure` and the `state_repo_plugins_tests.rs` fixtures.
- Make each test fully isolated (own TempDir, HOME, git env) so it passes under full-parallel runs.

# Origin
Observed during bl-5cc2 review (2026-05-21): the merged worktree's full `cargo test` failed this test on ~1/3 of runs while every other test — including bl-5cc2's own 36 — passed deterministically. The test file dates to bl-1098; the flake is unrelated to bl-5cc2's plugin-policy work.
title: Flaky: state_repo plugin-symlink tests race under parallel cargo test
